page: OVHcloud c3-320 is a compute-optimized server instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 160 dedicated physical cores on the x86_64 architecture, utilizing AMD EPYC processors running at a base clock speed of 2.3 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 320.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. Storage is provided via a local 400 GB NVMe SSD, and network connectivity is supported by a 20 Gbps baseline bandwidth.

In benchmark evaluations, the instance demonstrates strong multi-threaded capabilities. The stress-ng multi-core CPU test and PassMark CPU suite both place the server in the top 10% of tested systems. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, software compilation with Clang, and ray tracing workloads also achieve top-tier performance. Single-core execution, measured via stress-ng and Geekbench, falls within the top 25%. Memory performance is highly competitive, with small-block cached read bandwidth ranking in the top 10%, while large-block uncached read and write operations remain in the top 25%.

From a resource allocation perspective, the c3-320 offers high compute density with dedicated physical cores and no hyperthreading, which eliminates resource contention. However, the 2.0 GB of RAM per core represents a lower memory-to-CPU ratio, making it less optimal for memory-bound datasets. For database and key-value operations, the server achieves top-tier Redis SET throughput and strong PassMark database scores, supported by the local NVMe SSD. Qualitative cost efficiency is high for compute-heavy, parallelized workloads that can leverage the 160 dedicated cores without requiring massive memory footprints.

This instance is designed for highly parallelized, compute-intensive workloads. Suitable applications include software compilation, multi-threaded data decompression, ray tracing, and static web serving. It is also capable of handling database operations and medium-scale LLM prompt processing, though text generation speeds for larger models are moderate.
description: OVHcloud c3-320 is a compute-optimized server instance featuring 160 dedicated physical cores on an AMD EPYC x86_64 architecture running at 2.3 GHz. It is equipped with 320.0 GB of RAM, a 400 GB local NVMe SSD, and a 20 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Operating under the KVM hypervisor, this instance delivers top-tier multi-core CPU performance, ranking in the top 10% on stress-ng and PassMark benchmarks. It also achieves top-tier results in multi-threaded decompression, software compilation, and Redis SET operations. With a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core, the server is optimized for compute-heavy parallel workloads rather than memory-intensive applications. It is highly suitable for software compilation, ray tracing, static web serving, and database operations.
og_description: OVHcloud c3-320 is a compute-optimized server with 160 dedicated AMD EPYC cores, 320 GB RAM, and a 400 GB NVMe SSD, delivering top-tier multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth performance.

tagline: A compute-optimized platform featuring 160 dedicated physical cores and local NVMe storage for parallel processing workloads.
